# get-proto

[![github actions](https://img.shields.io/endpoint?url=https://github-actions-badge-u3jn4tfpocch.runkit.sh/ljharb/get-proto)](https://github.com/ljharb/get-proto/actions) [![coverage](https://codecov.io/gh/ljharb/get-proto/branch/main/graphs/badge.svg)](https://app.codecov.io/gh/ljharb/get-proto/) [![License](https://img.shields.io/npm/l/get-proto.svg)](../../../node_modules/get-proto/LICENSE/) [![Downloads](https://img.shields.io/npm/dm/get-proto.svg)](https://npm-stat.com/charts.html?package=get-proto)

[![npm badge](https://nodei.co/npm/get-proto.png?downloads=true\&stars=true)](https://npmjs.org/package/get-proto)

Robustly get the \[\[Prototype]] of an object. Uses the best available method.

## Getting started

```sh
npm install --save get-proto
```

## Usage/Examples

```js
const assert = require('assert');
const getProto = require('get-proto');

const a = { a: 1, b: 2, [Symbol.toStringTag]: 'foo' };
const b = { c: 3, __proto__: a };

assert.equal(getProto(b), a);
assert.equal(getProto(a), Object.prototype);
assert.equal(getProto({ __proto__: null }), null);
```

## Tests

Clone the repo, `npm install`, and run `npm test`
